commit_message:
IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements Hi @lawrie 

I would like to use your PIO implementation with the RISCV soft-core for implementing USB host functionality on the tangnano20k board from GOWIN. I noticed that not all required components are implemented, mainly the IRQ support. This series of patches implements IRQ and includes various synthesis warning fixes and improvements. This is my first experience in HDL, so ple

examples.commit_message:
IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements Hi @lawrie 

I would like to use your PIO implementation with the RISCV soft-core for implementing USB host functionality on the tangnano20k board from GOWIN. I noticed that not all required components are implemented, mainly the IRQ support. This series of patches implements IRQ and includes various synthesis warning fixes and improvements. This is my first experience in HDL, so ple
examples.patch:
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ VERILOG_FILES = ../src/pio.v ../src/machine.v ../src/decoder.v ../src/divider.v
 TB ?= tb
 
 ${TB}: *.v
-	iverilog -o tb ${TB}.v ${VERILOG_FILES}
+	iverilog -o tb ${TB}.v ${VERILOG_FILES} -I ../src
 
 sim: ${TB}
 	./tb
examples.before_code:
VERILOG_FILES = ../src/pio.v ../src/machine.v ../src/decoder.v ../src/divider.v ../src/pc.v \
                ../src/scratch.v ../src/fifo.v ../src/isr.v ../src/osr.v
TB ?= tb

${TB}: *.v
	iverilog -o tb ${TB}.v ${VERILOG_FILES}

sim: ${TB}
	./tb

clean:
	rm tb
examples.after_code:
VERILOG_FILES = ../src/pio.v ../src/machine.v ../src/decoder.v ../src/divider.v ../src/pc.v \
                ../src/scratch.v ../src/fifo.v ../src/isr.v ../src/osr.v
TB ?= tb

${TB}: *.v
	iverilog -o tb ${TB}.v ${VERILOG_FILES} -I ../src

sim: ${TB}
	./tb

clean:
	rm tb

examples.commit_message:
IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements Hi @lawrie 

I would like to use your PIO implementation with the RISCV soft-core for implementing USB host functionality on the tangnano20k board from GOWIN. I noticed that not all required components are implemented, mainly the IRQ support. This series of patches implements IRQ and includes various synthesis warning fixes and improvements. This is my first experience in HDL, so ple

examples.patch:
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@ module top (
   // PIO registers and wires
   reg [31:0]  din;        // Data sent to PIO
   reg [4:0]   index;      // Instruction index
-  reg [3:0]   action;     // Action to be done by PIO
+  reg [5:0]   action;     // Action to be done by PIO
   reg [1:0]   mindex;     // Machine index
   reg [31:0]  gpio_in;    // Input pins to PIO

examples.commit_message:
IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements IRQ support and various of other fixes and improvements Hi @lawrie 

I would like to use your PIO implementation with the RISCV soft-core for implementing USB host functionality on the tangnano20k board from GOWIN. I noticed that not all required components are implemented, mainly the IRQ support. This series of patches implements IRQ and includes various synthesis warning fixes and improvements. This is my first experience in HDL, so ple
examples.patch:
@@ -1,18 +1,17 @@
 `default_nettype none
 module top (
   input         clk_25mhz,
-  // Buttons
-  input [6:0]   btn,
-  // Leds
-  output [7:0]  led,
-  output [27:0] gn,
+  // Button
+  input         btn,
+  // Led
+  output [2:0]  led,
   output        tx
 );
 
   // PIO registers and wires
   reg [31:0]  din;        // Data sent to PIO
   reg [4:0]   index;      // Instruction index
-  reg [3:0]   action;     // Action to be done by PIO
+  reg [5:0]   action;     // Action to be done by PIO
   reg [1:0]   mindex;     // Machine index
   reg [31:0]  gpio_in;    // Input pins to PIO
